Add comprehensive JavaDoc comments to all classes and methods

commitResults.messageBody:
- Add DialogBox class for GUI chat bubbles
- Add ReiGui class as main GUI application
- Add Launcher class to launch GUI
- Add GuiUi class to handle GUI interactions
- Add UiInterface to abstract UI implementations
- Add GUI styles and images resources
- Update build.gradle.kts with JavaFX dependencies
- Modify Rei.java to support GUI mode
- Update command classes for GUI compatibility
- Add GUI unit tests

Added complete JavaFX GUI implementation including dialog boxes,
main GUI window, launcher, and updated architecture to support
both CLI and GUI interfaces.

commitResults.messageTitle: A-Assertions: Add assertions for better error handling and debugging
Merge branch 'branch-A-Assertions' - Add assertions for better error handling and debugging

commitResults.messageBody:
- Eliminated magic numbers by introducing named constants
- Broke down long methods into smaller, focused functions
- Reduced deep nesting and complex expressions
- Made code more explicit and readable
- Added proper error handling and validation
- Improved resource management with try-with-resources
- Enhanced method naming for better clarity
- Added explicit default case handling
- Separated concerns for better maintainability
- Improved layout and logical structure"
\A-CodeQuality: Comprehensive code quality improvements

commitResults.messageTitle: Add confirmation prompt for duplicate tasks
Implement JavaFX Alert dialogs for duplicate confirmation in GUI mode

commitResults.messageTitle: Restructure command flow with pending confirmation state for duplicates
Add GUI support for pending confirmation state
Fix escaped newline in GUI confirmation message
Add flexible data source feature with source command
Add confirmation prompt to create new data file if it doesn't exist
Fix source switching functionality with proper error handling
commitResults.messageBody:
- Added handleSourceCommand() in ReiGui to properly handle source switches in GUI mode
- Enhanced switchDataSource() with comprehensive validation and error handling
- Added try-catch blocks to surface exceptions instead of silent failures
- Improved error messages for better debugging and user feedback

commitResults.messageTitle: Add C-Sort: Ability to sort tasks chronologically
commitResults.messageBody:
- Added getDeadline() getter to Deadline class
- Added getStart() and getEnd() getters to Event class
- Created SortCommand to sort tasks by date
- Deadlines sorted by deadline date, Events by start date
- Todos placed at end and sorted alphabetically
- Added 'sort' command parsing to Parser
- Tasks are saved in sorted order after sorting

- Added insertion order tracking to Task base class
- Each task gets a sequential order number when created
- Updated SortCommand to support both chronological and insertion sorting
- Added sort command variants: 'sort insertion/original' and 'sort date/chronological'
- Default 'sort' command still sorts chronologically
- Users can now restore original task order with 'sort insertion'

commitResults.messageTitle: Add C-NaturalDates: Support natural language date formats
commitResults.messageBody:
- Created NaturalDateParser utility class for parsing natural dates
- Supports day names: Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat, Sun (full or abbreviated)
- Supports relative dates: today, tomorrow, yesterday
- Supports 'next' prefix: 'next Mon', 'next Friday'
- Can specify time after date: 'Mon 14:00', 'tomorrow 09:30'
- Falls back to standard yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm format if not natural date
- Integrated into existing Parser.parseDate() method
- Makes deadline and event creation more user-friendly

commitResults.messageTitle: Add flexible date format: support date-only input
commitResults.messageBody:
- Users can now omit time when entering dates (e.g., '2026-02-25')
- Date-only format defaults to 23:59 for end-of-day
- Still supports full format with time (yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm)
- Makes date input more convenient for users
- Updated Parser to try both formats automatically

commitResults.messageTitle: Add C-BetterSearch: Enhanced find command flexibility
commitResults.messageBody:
- Support multiple keywords in one search (space-separated)
- Matches if ANY keyword is found (OR logic)
- Search across task descriptions AND task types (todo, deadline, event)
- Case-insensitive partial matching (already supported, now enhanced)
- Shows count of matching tasks in results
- Example: 'find book deadline' finds all deadlines with 'book' in description OR all tasks with 'book'/'deadline' in them

commitResults.messageTitle: Add AND/OR search mode specification for find command
commitResults.messageBody:
- Users can now specify AND or OR logic in find commands
- Format: 'find AND keyword1 keyword2' or 'find OR keyword1 keyword2'
- AND mode: matches only if ALL keywords are found in task
- OR mode: matches if ANY keyword is found in task (default)
- Default behavior (no mode specified) remains OR for backward compatibility
- Results display shows which search mode was used
- Examples:
  * 'find book' - OR search (default)
  * 'find OR book report' - explicit OR search
  * 'find AND book report' - matches only tasks containing both 'book' AND 'report'

commitResults.messageTitle: Add C-Update: Edit task details and clone tasks
commitResults.messageBody:
- Added setter methods to Task, Deadline, and Event classes
- Created UpdateCommand to modify task details without deletion:
  * Update description: 'update 1 /desc new description'
  * Update deadline: 'update 2 /by 2026-03-01' (for Deadline tasks)
  * Update event times: 'update 3 /from Mon 09:00 /to Mon 17:00' (for Event tasks)
  * Can update start only, end only, or both for events
- Created CloneCommand to duplicate tasks:
  * Clone any task: 'clone 2'
  * Preserves description, dates, and done status
  * New clone gets fresh insertion order
- Added comprehensive parsing in Parser for both commands
- Validates task type compatibility (e.g., can't set deadline on Todo)
- All changes are automatically saved to storage

commitResults.messageTitle: Add C-Tagging: Support for tagging tasks with hashtags
commitResults.messageBody:
- Add tags field (HashSet<String>) to Task class
- Implement addTag(), removeTag(), getTags(), hasTag() methods
- Tags stored as lowercase without # prefix for consistency
- Tags display as '#tag1 #tag2' in toString()
- Tags saved as 'tag1,tag2' in toFileString()
- Create TagCommand to add tags to tasks (format: 'tag <index> <tag>')
- Create UntagCommand to remove tags from tasks (format: 'untag <index> <tag>')
- Update Parser to handle tag/untag commands
- Update Storage to load tags from file during task loading
- Update FindCommand to search task tags with AND/OR logic
- Support searching with or without # prefix (e.g., 'find #fun' or 'find fun')

commitResults.messageTitle: Add C-Priority: Support for task priority levels
commitResults.messageBody:
- Create Priority enum with HIGH(1), MEDIUM(2), LOW(3), NONE(0) levels
- Add priority field to Task class with getter/setter methods
- Priority displays as [HIGH]/[MEDIUM]/[LOW] in toString(), empty for NONE
- Priority saved as numeric level (0-3) in file storage format
- Update file format: status | priority | description (for all task types)
- Create PriorityCommand to set task priority
- Update Parser to handle priority command
  - Format: 'priority <index> <level>'
  - Accepts HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW or numeric 1/2/3
- Update Storage to load priority from file during task loading
- Update all data file indices to account for priority field
- All builds passing (9/9 tests)

commitResults.messageTitle: Improve error handling for invalid priority levels
commitResults.messageBody:
- Catch IllegalArgumentException when parsing invalid numeric priority levels
- Provide clear error messages indicating valid priority ranges
- Priority.fromLevel() now shows valid levels: 1 (HIGH), 2 (MEDIUM), 3 (LOW), 0 (NONE)
- Priority.fromString() now shows valid values: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, NONE
- Parser now properly handles invalid levels like 'priority 2 4' with helpful message

commitResults.messageTitle: Add C-Archive: Archive completed tasks to separate file
commitResults.messageBody:
- Create ArchiveCommand to move completed tasks to archive
- Archives all completed tasks to timestamped file (Rei_archive_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.txt)
- Archive file saved in same directory as current data file
- Removes archived tasks from active task list
- Updates and saves the cleaned task list
- Usage: 'archive' command (no parameters)
- Displays count of archived tasks and archive file location
- Handles case when no completed tasks exist
- All builds passing (9/9 tests)

commitResults.messageTitle: Refactor archive functionality with two distinct commands
commitResults.messageBody:
ARCHIVE command (clear entire list):
- Archives ALL tasks (completed and non-completed) to timestamped file
- Clears entire task list for a fresh start
- File format: Rei_archive_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.txt
- Usage: 'archive'

CLEAR command (record completed tasks):
- Removes only completed tasks from active list
- Appends completed tasks to persistent completed tasks file
- File format: Rei_completed.txt (persistent, continuous record)
- Each clear operation adds timestamp header for organization
- Usage: 'clear'

Changes:
- Modified ArchiveCommand to archive all tasks instead of just completed
- Created ClearCommand for recording completed task history
- Updated Parser to support both archive and clear commands
- All builds passing (9/9 tests)

commitResults.messageTitle: Normalize file paths to use forward slashes for consistent display
commitResults.messageBody:
- Add normalizePathDisplay() method to ArchiveCommand
- Add normalizePathDisplay() method to ClearCommand
- Convert File.separator to forward slashes (/) in output messages
- Ensures consistent cross-platform path display
- File operations still work correctly on all platforms (using File.separator internally)
